title: Braiding Sweetgrass: Indigenous Wisdom, Scientific Knowledge, and the Teachings of Plants
description: Potawatomi botanist Robin Wall Kimmerer weaves Indigenous plant knowledge with Western science, exploring reciprocity, gratitude, and responsibility to the natural world. Essential reference for Room 9 (Plants) content.
communityEndorsement: Author is an enrolled member of the Citizen Potawatomi Nation and a professor of environmental biology. Book draws on her own community knowledge. Widely endorsed by Indigenous scholars and educators.

title: The Mishomis Book: The Voice of the Ojibway
description: Cultural teachings and traditional knowledge of the Ojibway people, originally published by the Red School House in 1979. Now republished by University of Minnesota Press with community support.
communityEndorsement: Author Edward Benton-Banai is a Grand Chief of Three Fires Midewiwin Lodge. Published originally by Red School House, an Ojibway community organization. Re-publication with community involvement and approval.

title: The Birchbark House
description: An Ojibwe girl's life on Lake Superior island in 1847, drawing on oral tradition and historical research by enrolled Turtle Mountain Chippewa author Louise Erdrich. Depicts birchbark canoe construction, seasonal food practices, and traditional crafts.
communityEndorsement: Author Louise Erdrich is an enrolled member of the Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa. She owns and operates Birchbark Books, an Indigenous-focused bookstore in Minneapolis. The book is widely used in Indigenous education programs.

title: OCAP Principles: The First Nations Information Governance Centre
description: The First Nations Information Governance Centre's authoritative publication on the OCAP Principles (Ownership, Control, Access, Possession) governing First Nations data sovereignty and research governance.
communityEndorsement: Published directly by FNIGC, a First Nations-governed organization. OCAP is a registered trademark of FNIGC. This is a creator-published resource.

title: Six Seasons of the Asiniskow Ithiniw
description: Community-produced seasonal knowledge documentation from the Swampy Cree language communities, documenting the traditional six-season cycle and its relationship to heritage skills and land-based practices.
communityEndorsement: Produced directly by Swampy Cree community members through the FirstVoices platform, a community-owned digital archive for Indigenous language and cultural preservation.

title: Pilgrims of the Wild
description: Conservation memoir describing life in the boreal forest, beaver trapping traditions, and wilderness skills. Note: Author's Indigenous identity claims were later disputed; use alongside verified Indigenous sources and apply critical framing.
communityEndorsement: CAUTION: Author Grey Owl's claimed Ojibwe identity was posthumously revealed as fabricated. This work should only be used with explicit historical-critical framing. Cross-reference with verified Anishinaabe sources for any heritage skills content.

title: Seven Grandfather Teachings
description: Illustrated presentation of the Seven Sacred Teachings — love, respect, courage, honesty, wisdom, humility, and truth — foundational principles shared across many Anishinaabe and other First Nations communities.
communityEndorsement: Co-authored with Chief Robert Joseph, Hereditary Chief of the Gwawaenuk Kwakwaka'wakw Nation and Ambassador for Reconciliation Canada. Published by Orca Book Publishers who prioritize Indigenous authors and perspectives.

title: Tikinagan: The Spirit of the Forest
description: Documentation of Anishinaabe woodland skills and material culture including cradleboard construction (tikinagan), birchbark processing, and traditional woodland tools, written in both Ojibwe and English.
communityEndorsement: Author Patricia Ningewance Laverdure is an Ojibwe language teacher and elder from Lac Seul First Nation. Published through Mazinaate Press, a community-founded Indigenous language publisher.

title: Handbook of North American Indians, Volume 6: Subarctic
description: Authoritative scholarly reference for Subarctic First Nations peoples, cultures, languages, and heritage practices. Published by the Smithsonian Institution with contributions from community members and scholars.
communityEndorsement: Produced by the Smithsonian Institution with involvement from community representatives and scholars. Peer-reviewed academic standard reference widely used in Indigenous studies. Cultural sovereignty notes: this is a scholarly reference — community voices should also be consulted directly.

title: A Travelling Canoe: Canoeing and Cultural Revitalization in Tla-o-qui-aht First Nations
description: Community-produced documentation of traditional cedar canoe carving and paddling knowledge revitalization programs by the Tla-o-qui-aht First Nations of Vancouver Island.
communityEndorsement: Produced directly by the Tla-o-qui-aht First Nations Cultural Program as part of language and cultural revitalization. Community-owned intellectual property used with community authorization.
